title: 01219_Sequential_Underspecified_Instrument_Selection_for_Cause-Effect_Estimation
positive_examples: The proposed methods can help in understanding cause-effect relationships in high-dimensional systems, such as the influence of gut microbiome on health and disease, which can lead to improved healthcare outcomes.
The algorithm developed for sequential instrument selection can maximize information gain from experiments, potentially leading to more efficient and effective scientific research.
negative_examples: The reliance on instrumental variable techniques may lead to misinterpretation of causal relationships if the assumptions are not met, which could result in misguided healthcare decisions.
The paper highlights the challenge of unobserved confounding, which if not properly addressed, could lead to biased conclusions in scientific studies, impacting public health policies.
